name: WhisperX
pricing_notes: Fully open source; requires GPU for fast inference; run locally or on cloud GPU

auth.notes: Local library; HuggingFace token required for speaker diarization models (free)

performance.notes: ~70x realtime on A100 GPU (1h audio in <1min); faster than vanilla Whisper via batched inference

agent_readiness.notes: Enhanced Whisper with batched inference (70x faster), word-level timestamps, and speaker diarization (who said what). Self-hosted Python library. Uses CTranslate2 backend (faster-whisper integration). Better than vanilla Whisper for transcription pipelines needing timestamps and diarization. Research-grade software — not production-hardened.

gotchas: GPU required for fast inference — slow on CPU
Speaker diarization requires pyannote.audio (HuggingFace model access needed)
Research software — no stability guarantees, breaking changes in releases
Memory intensive for long audio files at large batch sizes
best_when: Transcription pipelines needing word-level timestamps for subtitle generation
Speaker diarization (who said what) from meeting recordings
High-volume batch transcription where faster-whisper is too slow
Self-hosted transcription without per-minute API costs
avoid_when: No GPU infrastructure (use AssemblyAI or Deepgram APIs instead)
Production applications needing SLA (research software, use commercial APIs)
summary: WhisperX is an enhanced Whisper implementation with 70x faster batch inference, word-level timestamps, and speaker diarization (who said what). Self-hosted Python library using CTranslate2/faster-whisper backend. Best for offline batch transcription pipelines needing speaker attribution and word timestamps without per-minute API costs. Requires GPU. Research-grade — use commercial APIs (AssemblyAI, Deepgram) for production SLA.
